docs.title: TAG Connect — Worker's guide
docs.text:
This guide covers everything in the TAG Connect app — from installing it to reaching the top profile tier. Every page is short and to the point. Find your question in the list, read that page, done.

(ਪੰਜਾਬੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਛੇਤੀ ਆ ਰਿਹਾ ਹੈ — Punjabi version coming soon.)

What the app does for you, in three lines: your cards, your ID and your experience live in one place, checked and confirmed by a real recruiter. Recruiters picking crews see a profile they can trust. The stronger and more up-to-date your profile, the faster work finds you.

The guide:

  • Getting started — install, sign up, say yes to the policies
  • Your setup checklist — the four steps that unlock your profile
  • Your cards — uploading, "being checked", fixing rejections, renewals
  • Your profile tier — the five levels, how to climb, why tiers drop
  • Your profile — personal details, work experience, skills
  • Messages — talking to your recruiter, the yellow action cards
  • Help & your account — getting help, notifications, leaving

Stuck right now? The most common questions: my card was rejected · what does "being checked" mean · why did my tier drop.

docs.title: Your cards
docs.text: Your cards are your proof — CSCS, CPCS, NPORS, IPAF, PASMA, safety certificates, the lot. This page covers their whole life in the app: uploading, getting checked, fixing rejections, and keeping them in date.

docs.title: Uploading a card
docs.text:
Profile → Cards → Add card. Photograph the front; the app asks for the back when it needs it.

The photo rules, worth repeating because they prevent almost every rejection:

  • Good light — daylight or a bright room, not the van at dusk
  • Flat on a table — not held in your hand
  • Whole card in frame — all four corners
  • No glare — tilt slightly if light bounces off the plastic
  • In focus — tap the card on screen to focus before shooting
SCREENSHOT wg-08-card-upload.png — the card photo screen with the frame guide

The app reads the card automatically — the type, your name, the numbers, the dates. You don't type any of that.

docs.title: "Being checked"
docs.text:
After upload, the card shows "being checked." A real recruiter is confirming the card is genuine, readable, and yours. There is nothing more for you to do — no need to message anyone or upload it again. It usually clears within a day.

While a card is being checked it doesn't count yet — your tier moves when the check completes, not when you upload. That's the whole point: everything on your profile is confirmed, which is why recruiters trust it.

docs.title: Rejected? Fix it fast
docs.text:
If a card comes back rejected, the app tells you exactly why, in plain words, with what to do about it:

The reason says Do this Photo wasn't clear enough Retake: better light, flat, in focus. Re-upload. We need the back too Photograph the back, upload both sides. This card has run out Renew the card in real life, then upload the new one. Details don't match Check the card is yours and your profile details are right — fix whichever is wrong. Wrong document Check what was asked for and upload that card. SCREENSHOT wg-10-rejected.png — a rejected card with the reason and Re-upload button

Tap Re-upload, send the better photo, and it goes straight back to be checked. A rejection costs you nothing except the retake — it doesn't mark your profile.

docs.title: Renewals and expiry
docs.text:
Cards run out — the app watches the dates so you don't have to:

  • Before a card expires, the app warns you. That's your cue to book the renewal.
  • When you get the new card, photograph and upload it straight away — you don't need to delete the old one, the app knows the new one takes over.
  • If a card expires with no renewal uploaded, it stops counting — and if it's your CSCS, your tier drops until the new one is confirmed. Upload the renewal and you climb straight back.

The habit that keeps your profile strong: the day a new card arrives in the post, it goes in the app. Thirty seconds, and your profile never has a gap.

docs.title: Uploading through your recruiter
docs.text: Handed your card to a recruiter in person, or emailed a photo? They can put it on your profile for you. It gets checked the same way as anything you upload yourself — you'll see it appear in your cards, "being checked" as usual.

docs.title: Install the app
docs.text: Get TAG Connect from the Google Play Store on your Android phone. It's free.

docs.title: Sign up
docs.text:
1. Open the app and enter your phone number.
  2. We text you a code. Type it in.
  3. That's your login — no password to remember, ever. Signing in again later works the same way: your number, a fresh code.

Keep your phone number current — it's your key to the app. If you change numbers, tell your recruiter before the old one goes dead.

docs.title: Say yes to the policies
docs.text:
Before anything else, the app shows you two documents: the Terms and the Privacy Policy. Open both and have a look — they're short, and they explain how we look after your details: what we store, who sees it, and your rights. Tick the box, tap continue.

Plain version of what they say: your documents are stored safely, recruiters see them to confirm you're qualified and legal to work, and you can ask for your data or delete your account whenever you want (how).

docs.title: What happens next
docs.text: The app shows your setup checklist — four steps that unlock your profile. That's the next page. The whole setup takes about ten minutes if your documents are to hand, so before you start, grab: your passport, your CSCS card, and — if you're not a UK or Irish citizen — your share code from GOV.UK.

docs.title: If sign-up goes wrong
docs.text: Problem Fix No code arrives Check signal, wait a minute, tap Resend. Still nothing — check the number you typed. "Code not valid" Codes expire fast. Request a fresh one and type it straight away. Wrong number saved Contact support — support@tagconstructionltd.co.uk — and we'll sort it.

docs.title: Getting help
docs.text:
• In the app: message support from the Help section — a real person answers.
  • Email: support@tagconstructionltd.co.uk
  • Quickest results: say what you were doing, what happened, and what you expected — one line each. A screenshot helps if something looks broken.

For the everyday questions, the answers are already in this guide: rejected card · being checked · tier dropped · share code trouble.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text: Settings → Notifications controls what the app pings you about. Our honest advice: keep messages and card expiry warnings on whatever else you tune out — those two are the ones that protect your work.

docs.title: Your data
docs.text: Your documents and details are stored securely and shown to recruiters for one purpose: confirming you're qualified and legal to work. You can ask us what we hold about you at any time — email support.

docs.title: Changing your phone number
docs.text: Your number is your login, so update it before the old one stops working: Settings → your details, or tell your recruiter. If you've already lost the old number, email support with your name and we'll verify you another way.

docs.title: Deleting your account
docs.text:
Settings → Delete my account. The app explains exactly what happens to your data before you confirm — read that screen, it's written for you, not for lawyers. Deleting is permanent for your access; some records are kept where the law requires it (employment and payroll rules), and the deletion screen spells that out.

If you're deleting because something's wrong or frustrating — tell support first. Most problems are ten minutes to fix, and we'd rather fix them.

docs.title: Messages
docs.text: Messages is where your recruiter reaches you — and where the app makes fixing things fast.

docs.title: Two kinds of message
docs.text:
Normal messages — plain chat. Read, reply, like any messaging app. You can type or use the mic to speak your reply.

Yellow action cards — the important ones. When your recruiter needs something specific — a better photo of a card, your date of birth confirmed, the back of a document — it arrives as a yellow card with one big button. The button takes you exactly where the job is: tap it, do the thing, done. No hunting through menus.

SCREENSHOT wg-14-action-card.png — a yellow action card with its one button

The golden rule: when a yellow card arrives, tap the button rather than typing a reply. Doing the action is the answer — the recruiter sees it's done the moment you finish, without you writing a word.

docs.title: What messages are about
docs.text: Most messages connect to something real on your profile — a specific card, a document, your details. The message says which one, so you're never guessing. If a recruiter asks about "your CPCS card," the yellow card's button opens that card's screen.

docs.title: Response habits that keep you working
docs.text:
• Same-day beats perfect. A quick fix today keeps you deployable; a perfect fix on Friday might not.
  • If you can't do it right now, a one-line reply — "on nights, will sort tomorrow" — keeps everyone happy.
  • If you don't understand what's being asked, say so in the thread. Plain question, plain answer.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text: The app pings you when a message or action arrives. Leave notifications on for messages at least — a re-upload request you don't see for a week is a week your profile sat weaker than it needed to be. You can tune what pings you in Settings.

docs.title: Your profile
docs.text: Beyond your cards, three parts of your profile do real work for you: your personal details, your work experience, and your skills. Together with confirmed cards, they're what push you to Site Strong.

docs.title: Personal details
docs.text:
The basics — address, National Insurance number, contact details. Filled in once during setup, then just kept current:

  • Moved house? Update your address the same week — site paperwork and payroll use it.
  • New phone number? Update it before the old one dies — it's your login.
  • Small corrections are fine any time; changes are noted properly on your record, so there's never a problem with fixing a typo.

docs.title: Work experience
docs.text:
Profile → Work experience → Add. For each past job: the site or company, what you did, and the dates. Rough dates are fine — "summer 2023 to spring 2024" beats leaving it empty.

Three or more entries is the target — that's the point where your experience fully counts towards your tier. List your most recent work first, and don't be shy about variety: groundworks, formwork, finishing — the range is part of the story.

Write it plainly. "Shuttering carpenter, 14 months, residential blocks" tells a recruiter everything; no fancy words needed.

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
Profile → Skills. A few honest lines about what you do and what you're good with: your trade, the plant you can operate, tickets you hold, the kind of sites you know.

Example: "Formwork carpenter, 8 years. Comfortable with table forms and traditional shutters. CPCS telehandler. Happy leading a small gang."

That's four lines and it does more for you than any buzzword. Keep it true — your cards and experience back it up, and that's the whole system: everything on your profile is something a recruiter can trust.

docs.title: Keeping the profile alive
docs.text: A profile that never changes goes quiet, and a long-quiet profile can slip a tier. You don't need to fuss over it — just let real life flow in: new card → upload it; job finished → add the entry; moved → update the address. A profile touched every couple of months stays sharp without ever feeling like homework.

docs.title: Your setup checklist
docs.text:
When you first sign in, the app shows four steps. Finish all four and your profile tier appears — that's the badge recruiters see when they're picking crews. Everything on this list is yours to do; nothing waits on anyone else.

SCREENSHOT wg-04-checklist.png — the checklist screen

docs.title: 1 · Identity uploaded
docs.text:
Tap Add. Photograph your passport — the page with your photo.

The photo rules (same for every document in the app): good light · flat on a table · all four corners in the shot · no glare, no fingers. Two seconds of care here saves a re-do later.

After you upload, the box ticks green with a small note: "We're checking it." That's normal — what it means. Nothing more for you to do on this step.

docs.title: 2 · Right to Work confirmed
docs.text:
This is about your legal permission to work in the UK.

  • UK or Irish citizen? Tap Confirm. Done — your passport is your proof.
  • Everyone else: enter your share code. That's a code you get free from the government at gov.uk/prove-right-to-work — it lets your recruiter check your work status. Takes five minutes online.

If the app says your code isn't right: codes expire after a while. Get a fresh one from the same GOV.UK page and enter that. This is the most common snag on the whole checklist — it's never a problem with you, just an out-of-date code.

docs.title: 3 · Personal details complete
docs.text: Tap Complete and fill in the basics — address, National Insurance number, and the rest of the form. Two minutes. This is what payroll and site paperwork need, so get it right once and forget about it.

docs.title: 4 · First card uploaded (CSCS)
docs.text:
Tap Add and photograph your CSCS card — front, and the back if the app asks. Same photo rules as your ID.

Your CSCS matters more than any other card in the app: it's the industry's baseline, and your profile can't reach the top tiers without a valid one.

docs.title: When all four are ticked
docs.text: Your tier badge appears — usually starting at New Hand, which just means "nothing confirmed yet." The moment a recruiter confirms your first document (usually within a day), you move up. From here, the tier page explains the climb.

docs.title: If something gets rejected during setup
docs.text: The box turns red with the reason in plain words — almost always the photo. Read the reason, retake the photo properly, tap Re-upload. The box goes back to "being checked." No harm done, happens all the time.

docs.title: Your profile tier
docs.text: Your tier is your profile's strength badge — one word that tells recruiters how complete and confirmed your profile is. It sits on your home screen, and recruiters see it when they're picking crews.

docs.title: The five levels
docs.text: Tier What it means New Hand Just starting — nothing confirmed yet. Every strong profile starts here. Site Starter Your first document has been confirmed by a recruiter. You're on the board. Site Ready Your ID and your CSCS are both confirmed. Recruiters searching for available workers usually start from here. Site Strong The full package, all confirmed: ID, a valid CSCS, two more cards, and your work experience written in. This is the tier that gets you picked first. Site Pro Coming later — it'll add confirmed references on top. Nobody's there yet.

docs.title: How to climb
docs.text:
Each of these moves you up when a recruiter confirms it:

  1. Your CSCS card — the big one. Upload it, get it confirmed.
  2. Your ID — passport confirmed (plus your share code where that applies).
  3. Two more cards — plant tickets, safety certs, whatever you hold.
  4. Your work experience — three past jobs written in (how).
  5. Your skills — a few lines about what you do.

Notice the pattern: uploading alone never moves you — confirmation does. A photo sitting "being checked" counts the moment it clears. And there are no shortcuts: the tier can't be bought, blagged, or bumped — which is exactly why recruiters trust it, and why yours is worth building.

One rule worth knowing: without a valid CSCS you can't reach Site Strong, no matter how full the rest of your profile is. Keep that card alive.

docs.title: Why tiers drop
docs.text:
Tiers move both ways. If yours dropped, it's one of these:

What happened The fix A card ran out — usually the CSCS Renew it, upload the new one. Once confirmed, you climb straight back. A document was removed from your profile Re-add a confirmed card and you're back. You've been quiet a while — months with no profile activity Any update wakes it up — renew a card, add experience, touch your details.

A drop is never a punishment and never permanent — it just means the profile stopped reflecting a fully-confirmed picture. The app tells you the reason, and the reason is always the fix.

docs.title: What recruiters see
docs.text: Recruiters see your tier, your confirmed cards, and your experience — the full professional picture. They do not see any number or score; the tier is the summary. If a recruiter mentions your tier, this page is what they're talking about.
